skill.name: wonda-cli
skill.description: Use the Wonda CLI for AI media generation, editing, research, social publishing, and platform automation workflows.
skill.summary: Guide agents through Wonda CLI media creation, editing, social research, and publishing workflows.
skill.author: degausai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified extensive command examples, networked workflows, credential handling, and filesystem use, but many individual hits are Markdown examples or benign documentation patterns. The skill still presents high marketplace risk because it asks agents to install and run a third-party CLI, manage passwords and session tokens, automate social platforms, use internal APIs, and publish or message on external accounts.

high_findings.title: Third-Party CLI Installation and Broad Command Execution
high_findings.description: The skill tells agents to install and run a community CLI through npm or Homebrew, then execute many local commands. This is a real external command risk even though most static hits are Markdown examples, because an agent may follow them directly on the user system.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are explicit install and command execution instructions. The commands serve the documented workflow, but they create real local execution risk for a community skill.

high_findings.title: Credential and Session Token Handling for External Services
high_findings.description: The skill instructs users and agents to set API keys, store platform passwords, retrieve decrypted credentials, and configure X, LinkedIn, and Reddit session tokens. This can expose account access if logs, prompts, shell history, or downstream tools mishandle values.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly documents API keys, passwords, decrypted credential retrieval, and session token setup. Legitimate use is possible, but the sensitive data flow is clear.

high_findings.title: Social Platform Automation with Anti-Detection Guidance
high_findings.description: The skill documents posting, following, connecting, messaging, voting, and DM automation across X, LinkedIn, and Reddit. It also references burner accounts, internal APIs, stealth browser automation, typing delays, and varied text to avoid detection.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections explicitly describe write actions and anti-detection behavior. This is a high-confidence abuse and platform-compliance risk, although not proof of malware.

medium_findings.title: Networked Scraping, Analytics, and Publishing by Design
medium_findings.description: The skill uses Wonda services and social platforms for competitor scraping, ad searches, analytics, media downloads, and publishing. These actions can transmit business data or user-generated media to third-party services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly networked workflows. The behavior is core functionality, so the risk depends on user consent and data sensitivity.

medium_findings.title: Remote Device Control and Signed Handoff URLs
medium_findings.description: The skill guides agents through creating remote devices, controlling social app flows, and sharing signed player URLs for human handoff. Misuse could expose account sessions or permit unintended actions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow is directly documented and includes remote device commands plus a signed URL. The text includes consent guidance, reducing but not eliminating risk.

medium_findings.title: Local Media Processing and File Writes
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends local ffmpeg operations, output downloads, overwrite flags, and writing analysis artifacts to temporary paths. This is expected for media work but can overwrite files or process sensitive local media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access examples are explicit and mostly operational. Risk is moderate because local files and generated outputs are central to the documented media workflow.

low_findings.title: Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged weak cryptography, but the visible context describes AES-256-GCM encrypted credential storage. AES-256-GCM is not weak by itself, so this specific pattern is a false positive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text names AES-256-GCM as an encryption mode for stored passwords. No weak algorithm use is evidenced at those lines.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings refer to billing guidance and example social URLs. These are not hidden exfiltration endpoints, although the skill does perform network actions through Wonda commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in user-facing examples and help text. No evidence shows covert data transfer to those specific URLs.

low_findings.title: Browser Credential File Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The scanner reported browser credential file access at line 52, but that line discusses consent and CAPTCHA handoff. No evidence found of direct browser credential file access in the cited context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains policy guidance, not a browser credential path or file read. This appears to be a scanner false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Executable Shell Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains many shell commands that an agent may execute directly, including installs, media processing, auth setup, scraping, publishing, and social engagement commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples are explicit executable commands in bash blocks. They are expected for a CLI guide, but they materially expand execution scope.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in Workflow Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Several examples capture command output into shell variables and pass it into later commands. This is normal CLI composition, but it can spread untrusted values if upstream command output is compromised.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present and operationally valid. The security impact depends on the trustworthiness of the CLI output and the surrounding shell environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Auth Material in CLI Workflows
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows API keys, passwords, auth tokens, cookies, and session values being supplied through environment variables or CLI commands. These values require strict handling controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The authentication material is explicitly named in the file. The workflow may be legitimate, but the exposure surface is concrete.

dangerous_patterns.title: Stealth and Human-Mimicking Automation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill describes real-browser automation, internal APIs, fingerprint mitigations, typing delays, and varied DM text. These patterns can enable account abuse and platform policy evasion.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited wording directly references stealth, fingerprint mitigations, and human-like behavior. This is a strong semantic risk beyond static pattern matching.

content.user_title: Create Media Campaigns with Wonda CLI
content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable ways to create, edit, research, and publish AI media from agent workflows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured Wonda CLI commands for end-to-end content operations.
actual_capabilities: Install, authenticate, and verify Wonda CLI access for agent-driven workflows.
Generate images, videos, music, speech, dialogue, and transcripts through Wonda commands.
Edit media with captions, overlays, cropping, audio replacement, transitions, and local ffmpeg routing.
Research competitors and trends across Instagram, TikTok, X, Reddit, LinkedIn, and ads data.
Publish media to connected Instagram and TikTok accounts and review publish history.
Automate X, LinkedIn, Reddit, and remote device workflows when authenticated accounts are available.
limitations: Requires the external Wonda CLI, account access, credits, and network connectivity.
Many workflows depend on third-party social platforms that may change behavior or restrict automation.
Credential, token, and account actions require explicit user approval and careful secret handling.
Some generation, analysis, and skill commands are limited by Wonda plan tiers.

use_cases.title: Launch Short-Form Product Ads
use_cases.description: Create product images, animate them into vertical videos, add captions, and publish drafts for review.

use_cases.title: Research Social Content Angles
use_cases.description: Collect competitor posts, ad examples, subreddit discussions, and X conversations before planning a campaign.

use_cases.title: Finish Media Assets Locally
use_cases.description: Download Wonda media, route deterministic edits through ffmpeg, and export delivery-ready files.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wonda CLI to create one 9:16 product image for this brief: [brief]. Ask before installing or authenticating.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Captioned Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a short vertical product video, add animated captions, and save the final file. Confirm the model, duration, and output path first.

prompt_templates.title: Research a Campaign Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wonda CLI research commands to compare Instagram, X, Reddit, LinkedIn, and ad trends for [topic]. Summarize useful angles only.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Safe Publishing Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Wonda CLI publishing workflow for [platform]. Do not post, message, vote, or accept permissions until I approve each action.

output_examples.input: Create a product video from a single product image.
output_examples.output: A concise workflow plan with model choice, upload step, video generation step, caption step, and final export path.

output_examples.input: Research competitors before creating a new campaign.
output: Content angles grouped by hook, format, and audience

output_examples.output: A checklist covering media ID, caption, privacy level, AIGC label, account selection, and final approval gate.

best_practices: Ask before installing Wonda CLI, authenticating accounts, spending credits, or publishing content.
Keep API keys, passwords, cookies, and session tokens out of prompts, logs, and shared outputs.
Use local ffmpeg only for deterministic edits after confirming input files and output paths.
anti_patterns: Do not automate consent, messaging, voting, following, or publishing without explicit user approval.
Do not paste raw session tokens, account passwords, or decrypted credentials into chat responses.
Do not use stealth automation or burner accounts to evade platform rules or user consent.

faq.question: What does this skill help agents do?
faq.answer: It provides Wonda CLI workflows for AI media generation, editing, social research, publishing, and platform automation.

faq.question: Does it require a Wonda account?
faq.answer: Some commands work anonymously, but generation, analysis, and advanced workflows require login, plan access, or credits.

faq.question: Can it publish directly to social platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill documents publishing commands. Agents should request explicit approval before any post or account action.

faq.question: Can it handle credentials?
faq.answer: Yes, it documents credential vault and session token workflows. These actions require strict secret handling and user consent.

faq.question: Why is the security risk high?
faq.answer: It combines external command execution, third-party network services, credentials, remote devices, and social account automation.

faq.question: Is the skill confirmed malicious?
faq.answer: No evidence found of confirmed malicious intent or prompt injection. It is high risk because its legitimate powers are broad.
